payload.summary: Inspected `src/adapters/fs.rs` and the related regression tests in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`. One amendment fixes a genuine diagnostic inconsistency. The other two would weaken intentionally conservative `plan_hash` handling that avoids incorrect lineage assignment under snapshot races and ambiguous legacy provenance.

votes.rationale: `unique_terminal_replay_match` currently returns `AppError::CorruptRecord` with `file: "unique_terminal_replay_match"`, which is a function name rather than a record path. In this module, other `CorruptRecord` errors consistently point at the underlying file, including `milestones/{id}/task-runs.ndjson`. The callers already have `milestone_id`, so switching this to the task-runs path is low-risk and improves operator diagnostics.

The missing start-time auto-population is intentional and technically justified. For a newly created row, copying the current snapshot hash into `plan_hash` can mislabel the run if dispatch happened under plan v1 but the milestone snapshot advanced to v2 before `record_task_run_start()` persisted the row. That would create a false `plan_hash` conflict when completion later reports the real dispatch-time hash. The current code preserves provenance in `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` and only fills `plan_hash` later when it can prove the snapshot has not changed.

Skipping backfill when `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` is `None` is the safer behavior. That state is ambiguous: it can mean either the entry predates any persisted plan or the row was written by older code that failed to record provenance. Backfilling such rows from the current milestone snapshot would silently assign a potentially newer plan version to historical runs. Without additional provenance, those rows are not safely repairable, so leaving `plan_hash` unset is correct.
